markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Miami be 100°F or higher on May 12?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Miami Intl Airport Station in degrees Fahrenheit on 12 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Miami Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/fl/miami/KMIA.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

events.description:

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects high uncertainty in short-range forecasts for Miami International Airport's May 12 high temperature, with equal 19% implied probabilities across 84-93°F bins driven by divergent National Weather Service guidance and model ensembles. Recent above-normal heat—highlighted by a 94°F record on May 2 and daily highs near 90-92°F through May 9—stems from a persistent subtropical ridge promoting sinking air and limited sea breeze influence, but isolated thunderstorms (20% chance per latest NWS outlooks) could cap peaks by reducing solar insolation. Differentiating factors include sea breeze timing, which tempers coastal readings while allowing inland spikes, and moisture levels favoring pop-up convection; GFS and ECMWF runs show 4-6°F spreads. New 12Z model updates and Monday NWS advisories will refine odds ahead of resolution.

markets.question: T20 Hong Kong Tri-Series, Women: Malaysia vs Hong Kong, China - Who wins the toss?
markets.description:
This market refers to the pre-match coin toss for the cricket match between Malaysia and Hong Kong, China scheduled for 2026-05-09 in T20 Hong Kong Tri-Series, Women.
This market resolves according to the official coin toss result (the team recorded as having won the toss) as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
If an official toss winner is recorded, the market will resolve based on that result even if the match is subsequently delayed, relocated, abandoned, or no ball is bowled.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market will remain open and resolve according to the toss result for the listed fixture when it is eventually played.
If the fixture is permanently canceled, forfeited before any toss is conducted, or otherwise completed without an official toss winner being recorded, the market will resolve 50-50.

events.title: T20 Hong Kong Tri-Series, Women: Malaysia vs Hong Kong, China
events.description:
This market refers to the cricket match between Malaysia and Hong Kong, China scheduled for May 9 2026 in T20 Hong Kong Tri-Series, Women.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.

If the match is postponed/rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed. If the match is permanently canceled or abandoned or otherwise is completed without a winner, the market resolves 50-50.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Hong Kong Women hold the edge in trader consensus following their dominant 31-run win over Malaysia Women earlier today in the T20I Tri-Series at Mission Road Ground, posting 136/4 with Natasha Miles (40*) and Yasmin Daswani (34) setting a challenging total defended by sharp bowling, restricting Malaysia to 105/5 despite Mas Elysa's gritty 51. Malaysia opened strongly by beating China by 26 runs, led by Nurin Imanina's 4/12, but face back-to-back fatigue ahead of the May 9 rematch. Home advantage, superior net run rate (+1.550 vs -0.125), and recent head-to-head success favor Hong Kong on a balanced pitch where toss and early wickets prove pivotal. No injury concerns reported.

markets.question: Game 2: Both Teams Destroy Inhibitors?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams destroy at least one enemy inhibitor during Game 2. In League of Legends, each team has three inhibitors (one in each lane), located behind the inner turrets.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Weibo Gaming and Ninjas in Pyjamas each destroy at least one enemy inhibitor during Game 2. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) do not destroy any enemy inhibitors.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 2 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams destroyed at least one inhibitor prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 2 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

events.title: LoL: Weibo Gaming vs Ninjas in Pyjamas (BO3) - LPL Group Ascend
events.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Weibo Gaming and Ninjas in Pyjamas in the LPL Group Ascend, initially scheduled for May 10 at 5:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Weibo Gaming" if Weibo Gaming win the match against Ninjas in Pyjamas.

This market will resolve to "Ninjas in Pyjamas" if Ninjas in Pyjamas win the match against Weibo Gaming.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: In LPL 2026 Split 2 Group Ascend double round-robin, mid-table Weibo Gaming (5-8, 14-18 games) hosts Ninjas in Pyjamas (5-7, 12-15) in a pivotal BO3 for top-4 knockout stage positioning, where both trail leaders BLG and TES by several matches. NIP holds the edge from their 2-1 head-to-head win on April 9, exploiting WBG's midgame lapses, but WBG rebounded with a gritty 2-1 over Team WE on May 7 amid roster tweaks like Hang in jungle and Meiko on support. NIP similarly halted a skid via 2-0 shutout of AL that day, fueled by Care's midlane dominance and Zhuo's roams. Patch 26.7 meta emphasizes early skirmishes, amplifying stylistic clashes in this evenly matched series with playoff implications looming.

markets.question: Will Tommy Fleetwood win the 2026 Truist Championship?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed player who wins the 2026 Truist Championship tournament.

If a listed player is eliminated from contention for the Truist Championship tournament based on the official rules of the tournament, the relevant market will immediately resolve to "No".

If an unlisted player wins the Truist Championship tournament this market will resolve to "Other".

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official winner as determined by PGA Tour official tournament rules. If multiple winners are announced then this market will resolve to the player whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

If no winner is announced by May 16, 2026 at 8:00PM ET this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source will be the official results published by the PGA Tour website (https://www.pgatour.com/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Alex Fitzpatrick holds a one-shot lead at 14-under after a bogey-free 64 in Saturday's third round at Quail Hollow Club, propelling his implied 32% probability atop trader consensus for the Truist Championship. Cameron Young sits two back at 12-under following a scorching 63, while Kristoffer Reitan lurks one stroke off the pace at 13-under in his strong PGA Tour rookie showing, compressing odds to 30% and 22% respectively for this tight final-round battle. Multiple pre-tournament favorites like Collin Morikawa and Jason Day withdrew amid injuries, opening the $20 million signature event field and amplifying the leaders' momentum on a demanding layout where driving distance and putting prowess could decide Sunday's outcome amid favorable scoring conditions.

eventMetadata.context_description: With Eurovision Song Contest 2026 underway in Vienna—host city after Austria's 2025 win—Polymarket traders heavily back Finland at 83% implied probability for Top 5 finish, propelled by Linda Lampenius and Pete Parkkonen's "Liekinheitin" dominating the OGAE fan poll last week and impressing in second rehearsals with live violin staging and high-energy visuals from their UMK national selection victory in February. Greece trails at 67% on Akylas's "Ferro" televote momentum, while Denmark and Israel show strength in Top 10 markets. All 35 entries' rehearsals wrapped days ago, but Semi-Final 1 tomorrow (Finland 7th) and Semi-Final 2 Thursday will determine qualifiers challenging Big 5 and host Austria in Saturday's grand final.

markets.question: Will Franco Colapinto be the 2026 F1 Drivers' Champion?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed driver that finishes 1st in the driver standings for the 2026 F1 season.

This market will resolve as soon as the official results of the final scheduled race of the 2026 F1 season are known.

If multiple drivers tie for first place in the drivers standings, this market will resolve according to the tiebreak procedure used by F1 to determine the 2026 F1 Drivers’ champion.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed driver to win the 2026 F1 Drivers Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Formula 1.

eventMetadata.context_description: Kimi Antonelli's commanding championship lead at 100 points stems from three consecutive Grand Prix victories in China, Japan, and Miami—capped by his pole-to-flag Miami win on May 3—showcasing Mercedes' superior race pace and qualifying edge over teammate George Russell, who won Australia but trails by 20 points after fading to fourth in Miami. Trader consensus reflects this Mercedes 1-2 dominance in the standings, yet keeps the duo tightly bunched at 37% and 29.5% implied probabilities due to intra-team rivalry risks, McLaren's rising threat via Norris and Piastri's recent podiums, and Red Bull's potential resurgence with Verstappen amid ongoing car development through the 24-race calendar.

eventMetadata.context_description: Dinamo București, sitting 4th in the SuperLiga Championship Group, hosts 6th-placed FC Argeș Pitești on May 10 at Dinamo Stadium in a key matchup for European qualification spots. The teams drew 1-1 in their reverse fixture on April 4, underscoring a tight head-to-head history where Dinamo leads 11-9 overall. Dinamo arrives in strong form after a 3-1 win over Rapid București last weekend, leveraging excellent home record (10 wins from 18) and recent momentum. Argeș boasts solid away results but faltered with a 0-1 loss to Universitatea Cluj prior to the draw; no major injuries reported for either side, though lineup confirmations and weather could sway trader consensus on goals or cards.

markets.question: Map 2: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 2 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 2 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 2, or if Map 2 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 2 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 2 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 2 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 2 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Imperial Academy advanced undefeated from Group A in United21 Season 47 with a 2-0 record, dominating LPH Gaming 2-1 and Sangal ALTERS 2-0 via strong showings on Inferno, Dust II, and Ancient, fueling trader optimism on their playoff momentum despite a lower HLTV world ranking of #193. Higher-ranked Infinite (#59) exited Group D in second at 2-1, overcoming AaB esport twice in Bo3 deciders but stumbling 0-2 to Misa Esports on Dust II and Nuke, highlighting inconsistency on select maps. Infinite boasts superior recent form, including a United21 Season 46 grand final win over BIG Academy, backed by rifler sl3nd (1.27 rating) and AWPer mhN1. A last-minute stand-in (fnl replacing Blytz) for Infinite adds uncertainty to this Bo3 upper bracket quarterfinal.

eventMetadata.context_description: Cremonese host relegation rivals Pisa in a crucial late-season Serie A six-pointer at Stadio Giovanni Zini, with both sides mired in the drop zone—Cremonese 18th on around 28 points after 33 matches, Pisa propping up the table on fewer. Cremonese's recent 0-0 draw at Torino extended their winless streak to four amid defensive resilience but attacking woes, exacerbated by injuries to striker Jamie Vardy (muscle, late April doubt) and Morten Thorsby (calf). Pisa slumped to a 1-2 home defeat against Genoa last weekend, winless in five with dismal away form (no victories in 17 road games), missing midfielder Marius Marin (knee) and defender Daniel Denoon (ankle). Pisa hold a recent head-to-head edge, winning 1-0 in November, but Cremonese's home advantage and desperation could shift trader consensus in this survival decider.

eventMetadata.context_description: Elena Rybakina leads trader consensus at 27% implied probability for the 2026 Women's Wimbledon title, buoyed by her 2022 grass-court Grand Slam triumph, career-high No. 2 ranking, Australian Open victory this year, and strong clay results like Madrid upsets over Ruse and Zheng Qinwen. Aryna Sabalenka trails closely at 24% with her world No. 1 status and baseline power suited to fast grass, but her recent Rome third-round exit amid injury concerns—requiring a medical timeout—has slightly cooled sentiment. Iga Świątek's 16.4% reflects her stunning 2025 Wimbledon bagel final over Amanda Anisimova, yet lingering questions about her grass adaptation versus clay dominance keep the top trio tightly bunched ahead of the June grass swing.

markets.question: Will Jorge Rodríguez be the de facto leader of Venezuela at the end of 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the individual who de facto holds and exercises the powers of the head of state of Venezuela on December 31, 2026 at 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, “de facto holds” refers to the individual who exercises primary executive authority over the Venezuelan state at that time, including effective control over the armed forces, national institutions, and core executive decision-making, regardless of formal title, constitutional designation, or international recognition.

Formal appointment, recognition by the United Nations, or recognition by foreign governments is not required.

If more than one individual claims to be head of state, this market will resolve to the individual who demonstrably exercises primary governing control within Venezuela’s territory at the specified time.

If no individual exercises effective governing control at the specified time, this market will resolve to “No Head of State”.

Indicators of de facto authority may include control over the armed forces and security services; control over executive ministries and state administrative institutions; enforcement of national laws; issuance of binding national directives; and effective control over the capital and core state infrastructure.

Symbolic status, foreign recognition without domestic control, nomination without effective authority, or expired prior service will not qualify.

The primary resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting assessing who exercises effective governing authority at the specified time.

Formal appointment, recognition by the United Nations, or recognition by foreign governments is not required.

If more than one individual claims to be head of state, this market will resolve to the individual who demonstrably exercises primary governing control within Venezuela’s territory at the specified time.

Indicators of de facto authority may include control over the armed forces and security services; control over executive ministries and state administrative institutions; enforcement of national laws; issuance of binding national directives; and effective control over the capital and core state infrastructure.

Symbolic status, foreign recognition without domestic control, nomination without effective authority, or expired prior service will not qualify.
If no individual exercises effective governing control at the specified time, this market will resolve to “No Head of State”.

The primary resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting assessing who exercises effective governing authority at the specified time.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Jakarta be 34°C on May 10?
markets.description:

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Halim Perdanakusuma Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/id/jakarta/WIHH.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: BMKG's latest hourly forecast, updated at 07:00 WIB, projects Jakarta's peak temperature at 34°C this afternoon amid partly cloudy skies and light winds, aligning with morning observations showing rapid warming to 30-32°C at key stations like Halim and Kemayoran. Urban heat island effects in the densely built environment amplify readings beyond regional models, consistent with early dry season patterns where May highs typically range 32-34°C per historical BMKG data. This strong model consensus and observational momentum underpin the 99.9% market-implied probability, representing trader confidence backed by real capital. Realistic challenges include afternoon convective showers cooling the boundary layer to cap at 33°C, or unexpected clearing boosting solar insolation toward 35°C; monitor BMKG's 12:00 WIB update and evening station reports for confirmation.

markets.question: Will Bulgaria advance through the second Eurovision Semi-Final?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the countries whose candidates for Eurovision 2026 advance according to the results of the Eurovision Second Semi-Final, scheduled for May 14, 2026.

If at any point it is impossible for the listed candidate to advance from the Eurovision 2026 Second Semi-Final based on the rules of the competition (i.e. they are eliminated), this market may immediately resolve to "No".

If this competition is not held or no contestants advance by June 30, 2026, 11:59PM ET, this market will resolve to "No."

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Eurovision (https://eurovision.tv/), including live footage of the Second Semi-Final for Eurovision 2026, however a consensus of credible reporting will suffice.

markets.question: Will 50-74 ships transit the Strait of Hormuz between April 27-May 3?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the total number of transit calls that IMF Portwatch reports for the Strait of Hormuz for all days from April 27, 2026, through May 3, 2026, inclusive.

Transit calls include container, dry bulk, roll-on/roll-off, general cargo, and tanker ships. Ships not reported by IMF Portwatch will not be considered.

This market will resolve as soon as data has been published for the final date in the specified period. If no data has been published for the final date of the specified period within 14 calendar days (ET) after the end of that period, this market will resolve based on data published up to that point.

Revisions to previously published data points made within this market’s timeframe will be considered. Revisions to previously published data points after data is published for the final date of the specified period, however, will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be IMF Portwatch, specifically the transit calls data published for the Strait of Hormuz at https://portwatch.imf.org/pages/cb5856222a5b4105adc6ee7e880a1730, both in the chart and through downloadable files.

series.title: ships transit the Strait of Hormuz

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket assigns a 98.2% implied probability to 25-49 ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz during April 27-May 3, 2026, driven by the ongoing 2026 US-Iran conflict and naval blockade that has slashed commercial traffic over 90% from pre-crisis averages of 100+ daily vessels. Real-time AIS trackers like Windward.ai report persistently low volumes—19 transits on April 25, 8 on April 26, and 13 on April 28—primarily limited to Iran-linked tankers amid attack risks and sanctions. This skin-in-the-game positioning reflects stalled ceasefire talks and over 1,000 vessels queued outside the strait. Realistic challenges include an abrupt de-escalation or escorted convoys boosting counts above 50, though IMF Portwatch data revisions due May 12 temper near-term shifts.

markets.question: Ehime FC vs. Kataller Toyama: Both Teams to Score
markets.description:
In the upcoming J2 100 Year Vision League game between Ehime FC and Kataller Toyama, scheduled for May 10 at 2:00 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Ehime FC and Kataller Toyama each score at least one goal during the game.

This market will resolve to "No" if either team fails to score (i.e., if one or both teams finish with zero goals).

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on jleague.jp. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Ehime FC hosts Kataller Toyama on May 10 in the J2/J3 100 Year Vision League West A at Ningineer Stadium, a rematch after their competitive 2-2 draw on March 8 that highlighted both teams' attacking potential and defensive vulnerabilities. Kataller Toyama sits higher at 2nd in the group standings with strong recent form, including wins over Tokushima Vortis and solid results against promotion challengers, while Ehime languishes in 8th amid a mixed run featuring a narrow 1-0 victory over FC Imabari but ongoing home struggles (1 win, 1 draw, 3 losses). No major injury updates from official reports disrupt key starting XIs, leaving home advantage and head-to-head parity—Kataller edges historical wins 4-3—as pivotal for trader consensus on outcomes like total goals or match result.

markets.question: Will Xi Jinping win the Nobel Peace Prize in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the 2026 Nobel Peace Prize, as announced by the Norwegian Nobel Committee.

If multiple listed individuals or entities jointly receive the prize, the market will resolve to a single winner. If Donald Trump, Volodymyr Zelenskyy,  Benjamin Netanyahu, Vladimir Putin, or Elon Musk are among the recipients, the market will resolve to the highest-ranked among them in that exact order of precedence. If none of those five are among the winners and the prize is awarded jointly to at least one listed individual and at least one listed organization, the market will resolve in favor of the individual. If no prioritized individuals are among the winners and all listed recipients are of the same type (all individuals or all organizations), the market will resolve to the person whose last name, or the entity’s name, comes first in alphabetical order as listed in this market.

If no official announcement regarding the 2026 Nobel Peace Prize has been made by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other."

The resolution source for this market will be the first official announcement from the Norwegian Nobel Committee.

events.description:

If none of those five are among the winners and the prize is awarded jointly to at least one listed individual and at least one listed organization, the market will resolve in favor of the individual. eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects a highly fragmented field for the 2026 Nobel Peace Prize following the Norwegian Nobel Committee's April 30 disclosure of 287 candidates—208 individuals and 79 organizations—leaving no dominant frontrunner amid diverse global narratives. Yulia Navalnaya edges ahead at 7.5% implied probability, buoyed by her ongoing role as a symbol of Russian opposition following Alexei Navalny's death, while Donald Trump's 6.5% stems from publicized nominations by leaders in Cambodia, Israel, and Pakistan touting his diplomatic interventions. Volodymyr Zelenskyy's 5.5% draws from his March nomination alongside Ukraine's people for defending democracy against aggression. With probabilities clustered below 8%, key swing factors include evolving geopolitical momentum and committee preferences for surprise picks, as seen in past upsets; the October 9 announcement looms as the decisive catalyst in this unpredictable awards landscape.

markets.question: Will Trump reduce the deficit before 2027?
markets.description:
The U.S. federal budget deficit for September 2025 was $197,949,630,362.16 (see: https://fiscaldata.treasury.gov/datasets/monthly-treasury-statement/summary-of-receipts-outlays-and-the-deficit-surplus-of-the-u-s-government). 

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Monthly Treasury Statement (MTS) reports a lower monthly deficit in December 2026 than in September 2025. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

The resolution source will be the Monthly Treasury Statement (MTS) published by the U.S. Department of the Treasury (fiscaldata.treasury.gov). The month surplus can be found in the column labeled "Current Month Deficit Surplus Amount" in the the table "Summary of Receipts, Outlays, and Surplus or Deficit” in the MTS (see: https://fiscaldata.treasury.gov/datasets/monthly-treasury-statement/summary-of-receipts-outlays-and-the-deficit-surplus-of-the-u-s-government).  If no report is published by February 28, 2027, 11:59 PM ET another credible source will be used.

events.title: Will Trump reduce the deficit before 2027?
events.description:
The U.S. federal budget deficit for September 2025 was $197,949,630,362.16 (see: https://fiscaldata.treasury.gov/datasets/monthly-treasury-statement/summary-of-receipts-outlays-and-the-deficit-surplus-of-the-u-s-government). 

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Monthly Treasury Statement (MTS) reports a lower monthly deficit in December 2026 than in September 2025. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

The resolution source will be the Monthly Treasury Statement (MTS) published by the U.S. Department of the Treasury (fiscaldata.treasury.gov). The month surplus can be found in the column labeled "Current Month Deficit Surplus Amount" in the the table "Summary of Receipts, Outlays, and Surplus or Deficit” in the MTS (see: https://fiscaldata.treasury.gov/datasets/monthly-treasury-statement/summary-of-receipts-outlays-and-the-deficit-surplus-of-the-u-s-government).  If no report is published by February 28, 2027, 11:59 PM ET another credible source will be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects an 84.5% implied probability for "No," driven by Congressional Budget Office projections showing the fiscal year 2026 federal deficit at $1.9 trillion (5.8% of GDP), with year-to-date figures already exceeding $1 trillion through February amid persistent revenue shortfalls and spending pressures. President Trump's policy mix—extension of 2017 tax cuts adding $1.4 trillion to decade-long deficits, alongside 42% defense spending hikes to $1.5 trillion in the April FY2027 budget proposal—offsets proposed non-defense discretionary cuts, per CBO analysis. Recent tariff policy shifts could further widen shortfalls by $1.1 trillion over ten years, reinforcing fiscal expansion. Key catalysts include final FY2026 Treasury data by September and debt ceiling negotiations.

eventMetadata.context_description: Estudiantes de La Plata enters this Liga Profesional Argentina Apertura Round of 16 matchup atop Group A with 31 points from 16 games and a +12 goal difference, bolstered by home advantage at Estadio Ciudad de La Plata and an unbeaten streak in recent head-to-heads against Racing Club, including a 1-1 draw and multiple wins over the past year. These absences compound Racing's mid-table form, tilting trader consensus toward the hosts amid playoff intensity.

markets.question: Game Handicap: PARI (-1.5) vs 1win (+1.5)
markets.description:
This market refers to the Dota 2 Upper bracket final match between 1win and PARIVISION in the 1win Essence Playoffs, initially scheduled for May 10 at 11:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "PARIVISION" if PARIVISION wins 2 or more games than 1win in this match.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "1win". 

Games won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the handicap, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching game being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.dotabuff.com. However, if https://www.dotabuff.com has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

events.title: Dota 2: 1win vs PARIVISION (BO3) - 1win Essence Playoffs
events.description:
This market refers to the Dota 2 Upper bracket final match between 1win and PARIVISION in the 1win Essence Playoffs, initially scheduled for May 10 at 11:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "1win" if 1win win the match against PARIVISION.

This market will resolve to "PARIVISION" if PARIVISION win the match against 1win.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.dotabuff.com. However, if https://www.dotabuff.com has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the 1win Essence I playoffs upper bracket final, 1win meets PARIVISION in a high-stakes BO3 series on May 10, with the winner advancing toward the grand final in the double-elimination bracket. 1win built momentum through an upset 2-1 quarterfinal win over favored Tundra Esports on May 8, followed by a dominant 2-0 semifinal rout of REKONIX on May 9, leveraging squad1x and v1olent's carry performances amid patch 7.41 meta favoring flexible drafts. PARIVISION, a direct playoff seed with Puppey coaching and Noticed standing in for SSS, swept L1GA Team 2-0 before edging PlayTime 2-1 in semis, relying on No[o]ne- and Satanic's mid-carry synergy. No roster changes or meta shifts noted; head-to-head history absent, emphasizing recent form and draft edges.

markets.question: Will the next Google Gemini model debut at a score of at least 1500?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the next Gemini model released by Google has at least the specified score on the Arena.AI Leaderboard (arena.ai/leaderboard/text) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab is checked on the date after the day of the release, 12:00 PM ET.  Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Results from the "Score" column under the "Text Arena | Overall" Leaderboard tab at https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text with style control off will be used to resolve this market.

If no qualifying score for the specified model is available on the Arena.AI Leaderboard at 12:00 PM ET following the date of the release, this market will resolve based on the first subsequent instance at which such a score becomes available on the leaderboard. If no qualifying score becomes available by the end of the seventh day following the day of the model’s release, or if no qualifying model release occurs by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

If multiple models are released on the same calendar date or if multiple variants of the qualifying model appear on the Arena.AI Leaderboard at the relevant check time (e.g., base, “Pro,” or “Flash”), the highest-scoring variant will be used for resolution.

A qualifying model must be launched and publicly accessible, including via open beta or open rolling waitlist signups. A closed beta or any form of private access will not suffice. The release must be either clearly defined and publicly announced as being accessible to the general public or otherwise made publicly accessible and explicitly labeled within the company’s official website. Labeling errors, placeholder text, or version names displayed on the website that do not correspond to a model that is actually accessible to the general public will not qualify.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at arena.ai/leaderboard/text. If this resolution source is unavailable at 12:00 PM ET following the date of the release, this market will resolve based on the first subsequent instance at which such a score becomes available on the leaderboard. If it remains unavailable through the end of the seventh day after a qualifying release, it will resolve to "No".

markets.question: Will "Mortal Kombat II" Opening Weekend Box Office be between 45m and 50m?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to how much "Mortal Kombat II" Opening Weekend Box Office will gross domestically on its opening weekend. The "Daily Box Office Performance" figures found on the “Box Office” tab on this movie's The Numbers (https://www.the-numbers.com/) page will be used to resolve this market once the values for the 3-day opening weekend (May 8 - May 10) are final (i.e., not studio estimates).

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Please note, this market will resolve according to the The Numbers figures provided under Weekend Box Office Performance for the 3-day weekend (which typically includes Thursday's previews), regardless of whether domestic refers to only the USA, or to USA and Canada, etc.

If there is ambiguity as to whether the resolution source's figures are final, this market will remain open until both https://www.boxofficemojo.com/ and https://www.the-numbers.com/ have confirmed their finalized figures.

If there is no final data available by May 17, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, another credible resolution source will be chosen.

markets.question: Will Letitia James be arrested before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed individual is arrested by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A qualifying arrest/detention includes:
* Being taken into physical custody by law enforcement authorities (including federal, state, local, military, or international law enforcement)
* Voluntarily surrendering to law enforcement authorities in response to an arrest warrant
* Being formally booked or processed following detention
* Being handcuffed by a state authority and taken to a police station
* Surrendering at a police station or courthouse with their attorney
* Being placed under house arrest or electronic monitoring

The following scenarios will NOT qualify as an arrest/detention
* An arrest warrant being issued but not executed
* Being briefly detained but not arrested (e.g. such as in the South Korean judicial system when an individual is awaiting a judge’s decision on whether to grant a detention warrant)
* Being questioned or interviewed without arrest
* Being named in an indictment without arrest

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the arresting government and law enforcement, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

The 2026 French Open is scheduled for May 18 - June 7, 2026.

This market will resolve to the player that wins the 2026 French Open Women’s Singles Tournament.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed player to win the 2026 French Open Women’s Singles Tournament per the rules of the tournament, the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the 2026 French Open Women’s Singles Tournament is cancelled, postponed after July 31, 2026, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the French Open (https://www.rolandgarros.com/en-us/); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors Aryna Sabalenka at 28.5% implied probability for the 2026 Roland Garros women's singles title, buoyed by her sustained World No. 1 ranking and powerful baseline game that has shown clay-court progress, including early Madrid wins before upsets to Hailey Baptiste and Sorana Cirstea. Iga Świątek trails closely at 23.0% despite her four prior French Open triumphs, as recent form dips—marked by a Madrid retirement and a gritty Rome opener against Caty McNally—have eroded confidence amid calls to "hit the panic button." Elena Rybakina (11.8%) and Coco Gauff (10.5%) benefit from strong clay swings, including Rybakina's Stuttgart title run, while Mirra Andreeva's Madrid final loss to champion Marta Kostyuk underscores the field's depth and upset potential on the slow red dirt.

markets.question: Will Zack Wheeler win the 2026 NL Cy Young Award?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the player who wins the 2026 National League Cy Young Award.

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official winner as determined by MLB rules. If multiple winners are announced then this market will resolve to the player whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

If the 2026 MLB season is cancelled, postponed after December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Major League Baseball; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Paul Skenes leads trader consensus for the 2026 NL Cy Young at 36.5% implied probability, building on his 2025 win with an elite 0.71 WHIP (NL-best) and 9.9 K/9 through eight starts, capped by a dominant outing striking out the side in the eighth on May 6 that reinforced his repeat potential amid low career ERAs against most opponents. Yoshinobu Yamamoto (16.5%) and Shohei Ohtani (15.4%) follow, buoyed by Dodgers rotation depth and strong early ERAs around 3.00, though Ohtani faces innings limits from his hitter duties. Cristopher Sánchez (9.5%) tops NL ERA at 2.39 with 60 strikeouts, Jacob Misiorowski (8.3%) ranks third in ERA at 2.63 as a Brewers breakout, and Chris Sale (8.0%) paces wins at 6-2 with a 2.20 ERA, reflecting early-season form and health sustaining momentum in a competitive race.

markets.question: Map 1: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 1, or if Map 1 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Counter-Strike: Spirit Academy vs Wave Esports (BO3) - Exort Series Main Stage
events.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Round 1 match between Spirit Academy and Wave Esports in the Exort Series Main Stage, initially scheduled for April 16 at 11:30AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Spirit Academy" if Spirit Academy win the match against Wave Esports.

This market will resolve to "Wave Esports" if Wave Esports win the match against Spirit Academy.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spirit Academy, Team Spirit's academy squad with a February 2026 roster refresh featuring Kiryasoo, VILBy, and s1nside, entered Exort Series #25 Main Stage Round 1 BO3 against Wave Esports as the implied favorite based on organizational backing and prior head-to-head edges in Exort events. Wave, HLTV #251 with March roster additions of Katalic, sheezy, and Belof, showed competitive qualifier form including 2-0 wins over BIG Academy and LEO but struggled with consistency on Ancient and Dust2. Traders weighed roster cohesion in the Swiss format—where 3-0 advances to playoffs—pistol rounds, and map pools amid a stable CS2 meta, with no major patch shifts or withdrawals reported pre-match.

markets.question: 1. FC Köln vs. 1. FC Heidenheim 1846: Both Teams to Score
markets.description:
In the upcoming Bundesliga game between 1. FC Köln and 1. FC Heidenheim 1846, scheduled for May 10 at 11:30 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both 1. FC Köln and 1. FC Heidenheim 1846 each score at least one goal during the game.

This market will resolve to "No" if either team fails to score (i.e., if one or both teams finish with zero goals).

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on bundesliga.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: 1. FC Köln vs. 1. FC Heidenheim 1846 - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Bundesliga game, scheduled for May 10 at 11:30 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: 1. FC Köln hosts bottom-of-the-table 1. FC Heidenheim 1846 in a pivotal late-season Bundesliga matchup at RheinEnergieStadion on May 10, with the visitors fighting relegation amid a dismal goal difference of -31 after 31 matches. Heidenheim boosted morale with a 2-0 home win over FC St. Pauli last weekend, extending a mixed run of W-L-W-D, while Köln slipped to a 1-2 defeat against Bayer Leverkusen at home, following draws and wins in prior games. Köln's defense remains depleted by injuries to center-backs Timo Hübers and Luca Kilian, plus striker Ragnar Ache sidelined for the season; Heidenheim misses forward Mikkel Kaufmann. Recent head-to-heads are tight, including January's 2-2 draw, underscoring Heidenheim's away upset potential despite Köln's home advantage and safer 14th-place standing. Both face demanding fixtures beforehand—Köln at Union Berlin, Heidenheim hosting Bayern Munich—potentially impacting fatigue and motivation.

If no 2026 Georgia Republican Senate Primary takes place, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be the first announcement of the results from the Georgia Republican party, however an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rep. Mike Collins holds a commanding trader consensus at 84.5% implied probability to win Georgia's Republican U.S. Senate primary on May 19, fueled by his sustained polling lead—33% to Derek Dooley's 22% and Buddy Carter's 14% in a May 6 survey—strong fundraising near $2 million, and appeal as a Trump-aligned conservative who passed the Laken Riley Act. Gov. Brian Kemp's recent campaigning for former coach Dooley, now at 15.3% odds, has spurred a polling surge amid attack ads and debates, including a sharp April 26 clash between Collins and Carter. Early voting since late April and Northeast Georgia campaign pushes intensify the race, with traders betting Collins secures the nomination outright or in a potential runoff.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Lucknow be 41°C on May 10?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Chaudhary Charan Singh Intl Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 10 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Chaudhary Charan Singh Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/in/lucknow/VILK.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: India Meteorological Department (IMD) forecasts a maximum temperature of 36°C for Lucknow on May 10 under mainly clear skies, with minimums around 25°C and relative humidity dropping to 40% by afternoon—conditions favoring solar heating but tempered by recent undershoots. Yesterday's observed high of 33.9°C fell short of the 34°C forecast amid a cooler-than-normal May pattern driven by earlier above-normal rainfall, boosting trader consensus to 82% implied probability for 35°C or below as crowds aggregate real-money bets on persistent moderation. Current mid-morning readings at 30°C with 18.5 km/h winds add uncertainty, as peak heating hinges on sustained clear conditions; real-time IMD observations will refine outcomes before daily resolution. Historical May averages exceed 40°C, but this season's climatological shift dominates sentiment.

markets.question: Will US unemployment reach at least 5.0% in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any seasonally adjusted unemployment rate (total unemployed, as a percent of the civilian labor force, official unemployment rate denoted as U-3) reported by the Bureau of Labor Statistics in an “Employment Situation Report” for a reference month in 2026 is greater than or equal to the listed percentage. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The relevant reports for this market are the Employment Situation Reports for January-December, 2026. This market may not resolve to “No” until the Employment Situation report for December 2026 is released. If no Employment Situation Report for December 2026 is released by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, however, this market will resolve based on all previously published data up to that time. 

The resolution source for this market is the Monthly Employment Situation Report, published by the BLS every month at https://www.bls.gov/bls/news-release/empsit.htm, specifically the U-3 measure in Table A-15 for each month.

Note: the resolution source for this market reports unemployment to one decimal point. Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

events.description:

Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

eventMetadata.context_description: The April 2026 Bureau of Labor Statistics report showed the US unemployment rate holding steady at 4.3 percent, with nonfarm payrolls rising a modest 115,000 amid slowing job growth in most sectors, reflecting a cooling labor market after stronger gains earlier in the year. Federal Open Market Committee projections from March peg the median rate at 4.4 percent by Q4 2026, aligning with economist consensus from the Philadelphia Fed Survey of Professional Forecasters at 4.5 percent, while CBO anticipates a peak near 4.6 percent amid subdued GDP growth near 1.8 percent. Traders should monitor the May jobs report on June 6 and the June FOMC meeting for signals on monetary policy responsiveness to persistent inflation above target, as further softening in job openings or rising jobless claims could push peak probabilities higher.

Alexander Volkanovski's featherweight throne reclamation bolsters his 29.3% share in this fluid rankings race.

markets.question: Will the Republicans win the 2028 US Presidential Election?
markets.description:
The 2028 United States presidential election will be held on Tuesday, November 7, 2028.

This market will resolve to the political party whose candidate is elected the next President of the United States in the 2028 election.

The resolution source for this market is the Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC. This market will resolve once all three sources call the race for the same party. If all three sources haven’t called the race for the same party by the inauguration date (January 20, 2029) this market will resolve based on who is inaugurated.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket gives Democrats a 60.5% implied probability of winning the 2028 US presidential election, with Republicans at 38.5%, reflecting growing dissatisfaction with the incumbent Republican administration amid policy reversals. Recent developments driving this shift include the Trump administration's decision to scale back promised mass deportations and de-escalate tensions short of war with Iran, eroding GOP support and fueling expectations of a Democratic wave in the 2026 midterms—where markets price House control at 87% and Senate at 55% for Democrats. With President Trump term-limited, the open-seat race amplifies historical patterns of opposition gains after eight years of unified party control, though upcoming primaries and midterm outcomes could alter trajectories.

markets.question: Abstract FDV above $200M one day after launch?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of Abstract's governance token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

The FDV will be determined using the total token supply multiplied by the token price.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If Abstract doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

eventMetadata.context_description: Jannik Sinner's trader consensus at 71.5% implied probability for the 2026 French Open reflects his dominant clay-court form amid Carlos Alcaraz's April 24 withdrawal due to a persistent right wrist injury, clearing a major rival and two-time defending champion from Roland Garros. World No. 1 Sinner, fresh off a record-breaking Masters 1000 streak including a straight-sets win over Alcaraz earlier on clay, advanced past Sebastian Ofner in Rome's second round on May 8, showcasing baseline prowess and improved drop shots suited to slow red clay. Alexander Zverev at 6% benefits from strong Roland Garros history as runner-up, while Rafael Jodar (3.4%) gains from rising ATP No. 34 status and Spanish clay pedigree; Novak Djokovic (3.8%) trails amid age-related concerns despite pedigree. French hopefuls like Arthur Fils and Lorenzo Musetti draw home support, but Sinner's surface adaptation and health edge define the wide market gap.

events.title: UFC Fight Night: Stephanie Luciano vs. Carol Foro (Women's Strawweight, Prelims)
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Stephanie Luciano" if Stephanie Luciano is officially declared the winner of the fight against Carol Foro at UFC Fight Night: Adesanya vs. Pyfer, scheduled for March 28, 2026.

It will resolve to "Carol Foro" if Carol Foro is officially declared the winner.

If the fight is declared a draw or technical draw, ruled a No Contest, not scored, canceled, or postponed beyond April 11, 2026, this market will resolve "50-50."

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the UFC.

markets.question: Will David Njoku play for Indianapolis Colts in 2026-27?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the next team David Njoku officially joins by August 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

If David Njoku does not officially join a new team by August 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

If David Njoku joins a team that is not listed, this market will resolve to “Other”.

If David Njoku is released, retires, or is not under contract with any professional team by August 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

An official signing announcement prior to the market’s close date will immediately resolve this market to the corresponding option.

The primary resolution sources for this market will be official announcements from the NFL and/or the acquiring team. A consensus of credible media reporting may also be used to resolve the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Free agent tight end David Njoku remains unsigned after the 2026 NFL Draft, fueling tightly clustered trader consensus around 45-49% implied probabilities for Atlanta Falcons, Cincinnati Bengals, Los Angeles Chargers, Tennessee Titans, Arizona Cardinals, and Indianapolis Colts, as no frontrunner has emerged in a wide-open market. His February Instagram post signaling a Browns exit—followed by Cleveland's veteran tight end signing—dropped their odds to 24.5%, despite his career 384 receptions for 4,062 yards and 34 touchdowns. Recent Chargers visit amid their thin tight end room post-draft boosted L.A., while Falcons lead on Kyle Pitts trade rumors creating scheme fit under ex-Browns coordinator influences; Bengals seek Burrow weapons, Titans eye Levis upgrade, and others leverage cap space for the 30-year-old Pro Bowler. This post-draft stasis underscores competitive free agency dynamics with multiple QB1 rosters needing pass-catching tight end production.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the PA-01 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the PA-01 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Rep. Brian Fitzpatrick (R) holds a commanding fundraising lead with over $7.6 million cash on hand entering the May 19 Democratic primary, where Bucks County Commissioner Bob Harvie leads Lucia Simonelli as the likely challenger in this D+1 battleground district pivotal to Democrats' House majority flip path amid Republicans' slim 218-214 edge. Trader consensus tilts slightly Democratic at 52.5% despite a March poll showing Fitzpatrick ahead 48-42 over Harvie, reflecting Fitzpatrick's recent bipartisan votes—including support for Haitian TPS extension on April 16 and Canada tariff repeal—that have drawn GOP primary ire and depressed base enthusiasm. Separation could arise from the primary outcome, national midterm headwinds, or heavy outside spending targeting Bucks County's swing voters.

markets.question: Will Elon Musk post 120-139 tweets from May 12 to May 19, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from May 12 12:00 PM ET to May 19, 2026 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

markets.question: Will Renan Santos finish in second place in the first round of the 2026 Brazilian presidential election?
markets.description:
A presidential election is scheduled to take place in Brazil on October 4, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate who receives the second-most valid votes in the first round of this election.

The named candidates will be primarily ranked by the number of valid votes received in the specified election. If two or more candidates are tied on valid votes, ties will be broken by alphabetical order of the candidates' last names. This market will resolve to the candidate that occupies the second-highest finishing position after applying this ranking.

If the result of this election isn't known definitively by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., https://dadosabertos.tse.jus.br/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent polls from Ideia, Real Time Big Data, and AtlasIntel in late April and early May consistently place President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva first in first-round voting intentions at 37-47%, with Senator Flávio Bolsonaro entrenched in second at 30-40%, well ahead of Ronaldo Caiado (3-6%), Romeu Zema (3-4%), and Renan Santos (under 5%). This polling trend, reflecting Flávio's rapid consolidation of right-wing support via the Bolsonaro family brand and absorption of votes from candidates like Tarcísio de Freitas who shifted to gubernatorial races, drives trader consensus pricing him at 66.5% for second place ahead of the October 4 first round. Lula's 19.5% odds capture the tight margins within error in some surveys, where challengers could overtake the incumbent.

markets.question: Total Kills Over/Under 62.5 in Game 2?
markets.description:
In the upcoming match between Nigma Galaxy and REKONIX in the 1win Essence Group B, initially scheduled for May 2 at 2:30PM ET:

This market is about the total kills in Game 2.

This market will resolve to "Over" if the total kills in Game 2 is 63 or more. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under".

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 begins but is not completed, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is remade, resolution will be based on the total kills in the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.dotabuff.com. However, if https://www.dotabuff.com has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.


In Dota 2, a "kill" for markets like team total kills, kill handicap, and interval kills is counted when an enemy hero dies and the game awards a hero kill event to the opposing team's score, which is the number shown on the in-game scoreboard and tracked in scoreboard feeds as team kills. In practical terms, this includes any enemy hero death that increments a team's kill score, regardless of who got the last hit, including situations where the victim dies to creeps, a tower, or neutrals as long as the kill is still credited to the enemy team. It does not include denies, such as killing your own allied units, and it also does not include building or objective destructions like towers, barracks, or Roshan, since those are tracked separately from kills. Assists also do not count as kills, because they are recorded as a separate player stat and multiple players can assist on a single kill.

events.title: Dota 2: Nigma Galaxy vs REKONIX (BO3) - 1win Essence Group B
events.description:
This market refers to the Dota 2 match between Nigma Galaxy and REKONIX in the 1win Essence Group B, initially scheduled for May 2 at 2:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Nigma Galaxy" if Nigma Galaxy win the match against REKONIX.

This market will resolve to "REKONIX" if REKONIX win the match against Nigma Galaxy.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.dotabuff.com. However, if https://www.dotabuff.com has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Nigma Galaxy entered their BO3 matchup against REKONIX in 1win Essence I Group B as undefeated frontrunners, riding a four-win streak in recent Tier 2 events and holding a top-20 world ranking with veteran execution in drafts and teamfights. REKONIX, an Indonesian upstart with a 2-2 group record prior, showed flashes in upsets like their 2-1 over Team Nemesis but struggled against elite macro play. No roster changes, player absences, or patch shifts disrupted the meta, emphasizing Nigma's home-region edge in online play and head-to-head stylistic matchup favoring their flexible hero pool. The series on May 2 highlighted group seeding stakes, with Nigma's momentum securing playoff positioning amid closely contested Tier 2 brackets.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the BetBoom Rise of Legends Season 10 2nd Division double round-robin group stage, The Soulless Team co-leads standings at 4-1 match record (9-2 games, 13 points) after a clean 2-0 sweep of Aterion Esports on May 1, building on four straight prior wins marred only by a narrow 1-2 loss to Dark Phoenix on April 30. Kings Crown sits seventh at 2-3 (4-7 games, 5 points), salvaging momentum with a 2-1 comeback over The World on May 1 but hampered by 0-2 defeats to top contenders HARD GRIND, Team Spirit Academy White, and Aterion. TST boasts perfect win rates on key meta heroes like Khaleed, Harley, and Sora, while KC struggles on picks such as Zhuxin and Kalea; this BO3 clash carries weight for closed qualifier seeding in the B-Tier circuit.

markets.question: Will Bert Mizusawa be the Republican nominee for Senate in Virginia?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Republican Primary for United States Senator from Virginia.

If no 2026 Virginia Republican Senate Primary takes place, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be the first announcement of the results from the Virginia Republican party, however an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

events.title: Virginia Republican Senate Primary Winner
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Republican Primary for United States Senator from Virginia.

If no 2026 Virginia Republican Senate Primary takes place, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be the first announcement of the results from the Virginia Republican party, however an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors retired Army Col. Bert Mizusawa at 45.5% to win Virginia's Republican U.S. Senate primary on August 4, reflecting a closely contested field ahead of the challenge to incumbent Democrat Mark Warner. A Public Sentiment Institute poll from May 1-5 showed Mizusawa leading narrowly at 23.5% over Kim Farington (22.3%) and David Williams (10.1%) among likely voters, but prediction market odds price Mizusawa higher, likely due to perceptions of stronger fundraising or veteran appeal in GOP primary electorates. The Republican Party of Virginia certified these three candidates for the ballot on April 7 after signature verification, while figures like Lt. Gov. Winsome Earle-Sears and Attorney General Jason Miyares hold minimal odds, signaling they are not active contenders. With three months until the primary, turnout among conservative base voters and any late endorsements could shift the balance.

markets.question: Will the lowest temperature in Miami be between 76-77°F on May 10?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the lowest temperature recorded at the Miami Intl Airport Station in degrees Fahrenheit on 10 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the lowest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Miami Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/fl/miami/KMIA.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.
